mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, it signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% within a 3-month window
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and declining housing starts indicates a rotation from cyclical construction to manufacturing efficiency, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistically significant difference between high INDPRO/low HOUST regimes and baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising real savings balances reflect precautionary accumulation that typically precedes a consumption pullback, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples adjust to higher fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth rates remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in industrial production signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which reduces input cost inflation and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances following INDPRO spikes above the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and stable federal funds rates indicates accommodative labor demand without monetary tightening, which historically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no significant alpha after controlling for PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS levels (F-stat p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.3% threshold.
